title: Why a French court ordered the historic retrial of a man convicted of four murders 30 years ago
subhead: Dany Leprince has long maintained his innocence regarding the murders of his brother, sister-in-law and two nieces at their home in 1994. Criminal retrials are extremely rare in France.

subhead: The lessons from the UK's unprecedented vote to leave the European Union 10 years ago have been all too quickly forgotten, writes Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon.

title: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' sparks a Homeric debate in Greece
subhead: The casting of a Black actress as Helen of Troy has ignited fierce controversy in Greece. Some lament what they see as Hollywood's appropriation of Greek mythology, while others emphasize the universal nature of these ancient stories.

title: Portugal, Spain and Switzerland reach World Cup last 16
subhead: Spain coasted past Austria on Thursday, thoroughly outclassing their opponents in a 3-0 knockout win, while Portugal defeated Croatia 2-1 and Switzerland beat Algeria 2-0.

title: Four graphs to visualize the unprecedented scale of France's June heatwave
subhead: France's most recent heatwave began on June 17 and reached unprecedented levels in terms of both peak temperatures and official alert status. It proved to be more intense than the infamous 2003 heatwave and could also surpass its duration.

subhead: 'America 250' (11/13). The Trump administration wants to restore 'truth and sanity' to the national narrative. In response to this rewriting of the past, in which neither slavery nor the dispossession of Native American lands are given their rightful place, the 'pro-truth' camp is getting organized.

title: Nearly 35,000 people became millionaires in France in 2025, UBS study finds
subhead: The number of millionaires worldwide has reached record highs, according to the report. Real estate and finance remain the most effective ways to build wealth.

subhead: A new generation of candidates, critical of elites, social injustice and US support for Israel, is making its mark in the Democratic primaries ahead of November's midterm elections.
